title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the work required to stop the cylinder, we need to consider both its translational and rotational kinetic energy.

title: Translational Kinetic Energy
text: The translational kinetic energy of the cylinder is given by the formula: $$KE_{trans}$$ $$=$$ $$\frac{1}{2} m v^2$$, where $$m$$ is the mass and v is the velocity.

title: Rotational Kinetic Energy
text: The rotational kinetic energy of the cylinder is given by the formula: $$KE_{rot}$$ $$=$$ $$\frac{1}{2} I {\omega}^2$$, where I is the moment of inertia and omega is the angular velocity.

title: Moment of Inertia for a Cylinder
text: The moment of inertia for a solid cylinder is I $$=$$ $$\frac{1}{2} m r^2$$.

title: Relating Linear and Angular Velocity
text: The angular velocity omega can be related to the linear velocity v by the formula: omega $$=$$ $$\frac{v}{r}$$.

title: Calculate Translational Kinetic Energy
text: Using the formula $$KE_{trans}$$ $$=$$ $$\frac{1}{2} m v^2$$, calculate the translational kinetic energy of the cylinder.

title: Calculate Rotational Kinetic Energy
text: Using the formula $$KE_{rot}$$ $$=$$ $$\frac{1}{2} I {\omega}^2$$ and the relation omega $$=$$ $$\frac{v}{r}$$, calculate the rotational kinetic energy of the cylinder.

title: Total Work Required
text: The total work required to stop the cylinder is the sum of its translational and rotational kinetic energies. Calculate this total work.
